Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed metaphorical clause: noun subject + intransitive verb in present/past agreement + illative noun; allows tense variation.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To become suddenly frightened, shocked, or emotionally frozen.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Pelästüä kovasti tai tulla hyvin levottomaks.
Usage Contexts: family talk; warnings; memories; storytelling; sudden news
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Uses cold and ice imagery familiar in northern Finnic rural life around winter, rivers, and unsafe travel.
First Example Sentence: Kun uutinen tuli joelt, ämmän süän männi jäähä, eikä hää heti puhunu.
Second Example Sentence: Miun süän männöö jäähä, jos lapsi ei palaa pimeän eellä kottii.
Third Example Sentence: Älä huuva niin kovaa; nuoremman süän männöö jäähä.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Modal verbal phrase: object noun + modal verb pittää + infinitive panna + illative/locative metaphor; object may take possessive context.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To keep silent, especially to protect a secret or avoid harmful speech.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Olla hiljaa ja olla kertomatta asiaa muille.
Usage Contexts: secrets; village gossip; family matters; workplace discretion
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects village-community norms where spoken news traveled quickly and discretion protected households.
First Example Sentence: Suu pittää panna lukkoo, ku pereen asia ei kuulu kylälle.
Second Example Sentence: Seppä tiesi hinnan, mutta pani suun lukkoo toril.
Third Example Sentence: Miä panen suun lukkoo, ennen ku sanon liikaa vihas.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Copular predicate phrase: infinitive/copula + genitive numeral phrase + noun + inessive postpositional noun; supports person and tense inflection.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be caught between two pressures, loyalties, choices, or opinions.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Olla epävarma kahen eri vaihtoehon tai ryhmän väliz.
Usage Contexts: politics; family decisions; workplace negotiations; migration choices
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Wind imagery fits coastal and lakeside Ingria, where weather and direction shaped travel, fishing, and field work.
First Example Sentence: Nuori opettaja on kahen tuulen väliz: jäävväänkö kylää vai männäkkö kaupunkii.
Second Example Sentence: Hää seisoi kahen tuulen väliz, ku vanhat ja nuoret tahtoivat eri tietä.
Third Example Sentence: Yritykses johtaja oli kahen tuulen väliz ennen sopimusta.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Balanced coordinate proverb: subject + present verb, subject + negative auxiliary + lexical verb; parallel clause structure.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Something is desired or visible but remains out of reach or beyond one’s ability.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Asia näyttää maholliselt, mutta sitä ei voi saaha tai tehä.
Usage Contexts: shopping; childhood; ambition; envy; practical advice
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Body-part parallelism is common in Finnic-style proverbial speech and suits oral teaching.
First Example Sentence: Lapsel silmä näkköö, käzi ei yllä, ku kukka kasvaa aidan takan.
Second Example Sentence: Miul oli kaupas sama tunne: silmä näkköö, käzi ei yllä.
Third Example Sentence: Älä lupaa liikaa, jos jo tiedät, jotta silmä näkköö, käzi ei yllä.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative proverbial clause: negative auxiliary + noun subject + finite verb + adjective-noun object; mostly fixed wording.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Old actions, relationships, or truths cannot stay hidden forever.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Vanha asia tulee lopulta ilmi tai jää ihmisten muistoo.
Usage Contexts: memory; moral lessons; local history; family stories; accusations
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Winter landscape imagery links visible tracks, snow cover, and community memory in northern village settings.
First Example Sentence: Ei lumi peitä vanhaa polkua; kylä muistaa, kuka autto hädäs.
Second Example Sentence: Kirje löytyi arkust, ja taas nähtii, ettei lumi peitä vanhaa polkua.
Third Example Sentence: Kun meez kielsi tekonsa, ämmä sanoi hiljaa: ei lumi peitä vanhaa polkua.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Verb phrase: transitive verb + partitive object + inessive instrument noun; may occur as infinitive or finite predicate.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To do useless work that cannot succeed because the method is wrong.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Tehä turhaa työtä, joka ei anna tulosta.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Domestic tool imagery reflects household labor and practical rural evaluations of wasteful effort.
First Example Sentence: Hää kantaa vettä seulaz, jos korjaa kattoa vain sanoin.
Second Example Sentence: Ilman opettajaa tämä työ on ku kantaa vettä seulaz.
Third Example Sentence: Miä en taho kantaa vettä seulaz; kirjuta sääntö ensin selvää.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Comparative simile idiom: infinitive/finite speech verb + ku-comparison + noun phrase in adessive location.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To speak clumsily, helplessly, or without confidence in an unfamiliar situation.
Usage Contexts: public speaking; school; official meetings; humor; embarrassment
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Fish and dry-land contrast draws on river, lake, and coastal livelihoods familiar to Ingrian communities.
First Example Sentence: Kun minult kysyttii laista, miä puhuin ku kala kuivalla maalla.
Second Example Sentence: Poika osaa laulut, mutta vieraitten eez puhuu ku kala kuivalla maalla.
Third Example Sentence: Älä pelkää kokkousta; muutoin siä puhut ku kala kuivalla maalla.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative gnomic clause: noun subject + negative auxiliary + motion verb + genitive/adjectival possessor + illative noun.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Good results, livelihood, or skill require effort; laziness brings nothing.
Usage Contexts: education; family advice; workplace discipline; farming; language study
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Bread imagery reflects agrarian labor, household subsistence, and moral teaching through food metaphors.
First Example Sentence: Leipä ei tule laiskan kätee, sanoi izä, ja vei pojan pellolle.
Second Example Sentence: Jos tahot osata ižoran keelt, muista: leipä ei tule laiskan kätee.
Third Example Sentence: Työpaikal kaikki tietää, ettei leipä tule laiskan kätee.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Metaphorical existential clause: animal noun subject + intransitive posture verb + adessive mental-state noun; accepts possessive/dative context.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To feel gloomy, suspicious, brooding, or weighed down by dark thoughts.
Usage Contexts: literature; personal emotion; weather talk; sarcasm; storytelling
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: The raven/crow figure echoes northern forest imagery and folk associations of dark birds with omens and worry.
First Example Sentence: Tänäpään hänel korppi istuu mielel, sillä sanoma oli raskas.
Second Example Sentence: Kun sade jatkuu viikon, kyläl korppi istuu mielel.
Third Example Sentence: Älä anna korpin istua mielel; lähme joelle ja puhumma.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative impersonal/passive-style clause: partitive noun object/fronted topic + negative auxiliary + passive verb + illative noun.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: An obvious truth, large event, or shared feeling cannot be hidden or controlled.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Selvää ja suurta asiaa ei voi salata.
Usage Contexts: politics; public scandals; family secrets; journalism; moral commentary
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: River imagery fits the waterways of Ingria and frames truth as something too large and moving to contain.
First Example Sentence: Jokea ei panna säkkii; kylä saa tietää suuren uutisen.
Second Example Sentence: Kun totuus on kaikkien silmissä, jokea ei panna säkkii.
Third Example Sentence: Kirjuta asia auki, sillä jokea ei panna säkkii.
